rulings.Case Title: BRUCE VANDERHOFF et al VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for November 3, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Caterpillar Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LYONS VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for November 3, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 4. Specially appearing defendant Brenntag Specialties, Inc.'s ("BSI") motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted. Plaintiff did not claim general jurisdiction and plaintiff failed to meet her burden of demonstrating that this Court may exercise specific jurisdiction over defendant. Plaintiff asserts that defendant is successor-in-interest to Whittaker, Clark & Daniels, Inc. ("WCD") but plaintiff failed to demonstrate specific jurisdiction as to WCD for the reasons stated in the order granting WCD's motion to quash. However, even if plaintiff had demonstrated that this Court may exercise specific jurisdiction over WCD, plaintiff still did not demonstrate that this Court may exercise specific jurisdiction over BSI because plaintiff did not demonstrate that BSI is successor-in-interest to WCD or that BSI assumed WCD's liabilities. Defendant's objections to the evidence submitted with plaintiff's initial opposition are overruled as to Exhibits B, D and E and sustained as to Exhibits C and F. Defendant's objections to the evidence submitted with plaintiff's supplemental opposition are overruled as to Exhibit C and sustained as to Exhibits D and E. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LYONS VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash By Specially Appearing Defendant Whittaker, Clark And Daniels Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for November 3, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 4. Specially appearing defendant Whittaker, Clark & Daniels, Inc.'s motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted. Plaintiff did not claim general jurisdiction and plaintiff failed to meet her burden of demonstrating that this Court may exercise specific jurisdiction over defendant. Even assuming plaintiff demonstrated that defendant has purposefully directed its activities at California, plaintiff failed to demonstrate that her claims arise out of or are related to these forum-directed activities. Because plaintiff failed to meet her initial burden, defendant had no burden to show that the exercise of jurisdiction would be unreasonable. Defendant's objections to the evidence submitted with plaintiff's initial opposition are overruled as to Exhibits B, D and E and sustained as to Exhibits C and F. Defendant's objections to the evidence submitted with plaintiff's supplemental opposition are overruled as to Exhibit C and sustained as to Exhibits D and E. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
